title: carya ovalis var. australis
intro: Carya ovalis var. australis, also known as the eastern black walnut, is a variety of tree native to eastern North America, characterized by its dark brown or black hull and edible nuts. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its high content of juglone, a natural compound with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Carya ovalis var. australis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of extracts, tinctures, and teas, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
